title: Create a Movie Collection Manager using Django ORM

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you'll will build a small Movie Collection Manager app to track movies in their database. This exercise focuses on using Django's ORM to add new records and render them in a simple HTML template.

The `models.py` file has already been created for you. Check it out to see the structure of the database schema. \
The templates files and url linking also have been done.

### Task
Complete the `views.py` file by writing the `add_movie` view function. This view should:
1. Create a new movie with the following details:
   - Title: "Inception"
   - Director: "Christopher Nolan"
   - Release Year: 2010
   - IMDB ID: "tt1375666"
2. Save the movie to the database using the .save() method.
3. Retrieve all movie records from the database. (already done for you)
4. Render the `movies.html` template with the newly added movie and the full movie list. \
The `home` view has already been rendered for you.

Click on 'Run' to view the home page. \
Click on 'Add a movie' to create the movie in the database and get the following output.

### Expected output
When the user visits http://some-url/add-movie/, or clicks on the `Add a Movie` link they should see:
